Dünya genelinde 1 Mayıs mitinglerinde işçi hakları ve sosyal adalet talepleri
May Day rallies worldwide demand workers' rights and social justice
3 src · 8 it2026-05-01 22:14
People's Dispatch · BreakThrough News · Brasil de Fato · Brasil de Fato · People's Dispatch · Brasil de Fato · BreakThrough News · Brasil de Fato
▸ sentez / synthesis
Avrupa, Amerika ve Güney Asya'da yüz binlerce kişi 1 Mayıs gösterilerine katılarak barış, sosyal adalet ve sömürücü çalışma koşullarının sona ermesini talep etti. Brezilya'da işçiler 6x1 çalışma düzeninin kaldırılmasını isterken, Küba'da yarım milyondan fazla kişi Havana'da yürüdü. ABD'de Madison okulları göçmen öğrencilerle dayanışma için kapandı ve Los Angeles'ta 10.000'den fazla kişi yürüdü.
Hundreds of thousands participated in May Day demonstrations across Europe, the Americas, and South Asia, calling for peace, social justice, and an end to exploitative labor practices. In Brazil, workers demanded the abolition of the 6x1 work schedule, while in Cuba, over half a million marched in Havana. In the United States, Madison schools closed in solidarity with immigrant students, and over 10,000 marched in Los Angeles.

The Onion's CEO Ben Collins, new owner of Infowars, shut down the outlet after nearly 30 years of broadcasts, expressing relief at the departure of founder Alex Jones, whom he called 'the world's worst rubbish.'

President Trump signed a key permit for the Bridger Pipeline Expansion project, dubbed 'Keystone Light,' which would transport Canadian tar sands oil along a portion of the canceled Keystone XL route. Environmental groups criticized the decision.

1916'da Danimarka Batı Hint Adaları'nda siyah işçi örgütleyicisi David Hamilton Jackson liderliğindeki kitlesel grev, Danimarka işçi hareketinin desteğiyle güç dengelerini değiştirdi. Olay, nadir bir işçi enternasyonalizmi örneği olarak vurgulanıyor.
In 1916, a mass strike led by black labor organizer David Hamilton Jackson in the Danish West Indies overturned power structures, with support from Denmark's labor movement. The event is highlighted as a rare example of labor internationalism.

CNN political reporter John King said that President Donald Trump's war in Iran is causing rural voters in Ohio to turn against him, making the state competitive for the first time in a decade. Polls show 61% of Americans oppose the military action.

On May Day, Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson and UAW President Shawn Fain called for international labor solidarity and a general strike. Rallies took place globally, from Buenos Aires to Istanbul, protesting billionaire wealth and labor reforms.
İran Savaşı Maliyetleri ve Kamuoyu: Anketler Çoğunluğun Bunu Hata Olarak Gördüğünü Gösteriyor
Iran War Costs and Public Opinion: Polls Show Majority Deem It a Mistake
3 src · 4 it2026-05-01 18:23
DropSite News · Truthout · Asia Times · Asia Times
▸ sentez / synthesis
Birden fazla rapor, Amerikalıların çoğunluğunun İran savaşını bir hata olarak gördüğünü gösteriyor; Washington Post/ABC News/Ipsos anketi %61 oranında onaylamama olduğunu ortaya koyuyor. Analistler, Pentagon'un maliyet tahminlerini sorguluyor; bazıları ABD vergi mükelleflerine gerçek maliyetin 25 milyar doları aştığını öne sürüyor. Uzmanlar, Trump'ın İran petrol ihracatını durdurma stratejisinin başarılı olmadığını söylüyor.
Multiple reports indicate that a majority of Americans view the Iran war as a mistake, with a Washington Post/ABC News/Ipsos poll showing 61% disapproval. Analysts question the Pentagon's cost estimates, with some suggesting the true cost to U.S. taxpayers exceeds $25 billion. Experts say Trump's strategy to halt Iranian oil exports is not succeeding.

Michael Wolff, a longtime reporter and author of books on Trump, said the president is likely to make things 'even uglier' as legal and political pressures mount.

Outgoing Massachusetts Teachers Association president Max Page reflects on a decade of reform, where rank-and-file members turned the union into a militant, member-led force through strikes and stronger contracts. The union also pushed for taxing the rich.

CCTV footage shows an Israeli settler pushing a 48-year-old French Christian nun to the ground and kicking her in East Jerusalem's Old City. Another settler spat at the entrance of St. James Cathedral.

The Wall Street Journal reported that Iran may be using mine-carrying dolphins to counter a US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, at least 20,000 seafarers remain stranded in the combat zone, facing physical dangers and psychological stress.

A survivor imprisoned in Texas reports being allowed to watch only Fox News, which they describe as force-feeding content that uplifts white Christian nationalism. The account is published by Truthout.

Nobel laureate economist Paul Krugman argued that President Trump and the GOP are dealing with an affordability crisis by denying reality rather than solving it, as Trump promised to cut prices on 'day one'.

Washington Post din editörü Matthew Schmitz'in haberine göre, daha önce Müslümanlara karşı çıkan aşırı sağ MAGA etkileyicileri şimdi İslam'a ilgi gösteriyor.
According to a Washington Post report by religion editor Matthew Schmitz, far-right MAGA influencers who previously railed against Muslims are now expressing interest in Islam.
Musk, Öldürücü Yapay Zeka Konusunda Uyarırken Ölümcül Yapay Zeka Teknolojisinden Kar Ediyor

Elon Musk has warned about the dangers of killer AI in his lawsuit against OpenAI, but critics note that Silicon Valley, including Musk, profits from AI that is already causing deaths. The Intercept reports on the contradiction.

On April 25, President Donald Trump survived a third assassination attempt when a gunman entered the White House Correspondents Association Dinner at the Washington Hilton. Secret Service agents tackled and disarmed the suspect.

Barbara Kopple's films 'Harlan County USA' and 'American Dream' captured labor struggles on picket lines and inside unions. Decades later, they are considered among the finest labor documentaries ever made.

On May 1, 2026, Israel killed over 30 people in Lebanon. Displaced Palestinians burned tents in protest of conditions, and Mali rebels called for a nationwide uprising. DropSite News reports on the day's events.

Truthout reports that Palestinians observed May Day this year against a backdrop of severe labor market devastation caused by years of war and genocide.

In a Truthout article, organizer Daniel Gross argues that effective and sustainable union building requires bottom-up worker involvement rather than top-down leadership from labor officials.

Jacobin reports that in France, a proposal to keep some businesses open on May Day has prompted unions to defend the tradition of a day off for all workers.

A whistleblower has alleged that the U.S. Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to rush the indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC). Common Cause issued a statement supporting the SPLC, calling the DOJ's action part of a campaign of intimidation.

Lawmakers are questioning the 'murky legal status' of an arrangement between Trump officials and top allies. President Trump announced that Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff would resume Iran peace talks, but later called off the trip.
2025'te üst düzey CEO maaşları işçi maaşlarından 20 kat daha hızlı arttı
Top CEO pay increased 20 times faster than workers' pay in 2025
1 src · 1 it2026-05-01 13:51
Common Dreams
▸ sentez / synthesis
Bir rapora göre, 2019 ile 2025 arasında küresel reel işçi ücretleri yüzde 12 düşerken reel CEO maaşları yüzde 54 arttı. En az dört CEO 2025'te 100 milyon doların üzerinde kazanırken, Broadcom CEO'su Hock Tan 205 milyon doların üzerinde kazançla başı çekti.
A report indicates that global real worker pay fell 12 percent while real CEO pay surged 54 percent between 2019 and 2025. At least four CEOs earned over $100 million in 2025, with Broadcom CEO Hock Tan leading at over $205 million.

Miles Taylor, a former Trump administration official, stated that many Republican lawmakers are fed up with President Trump but are scared to speak up. He explained why insiders remain silent.

On International Workers' Day, Liberation News reported that millions of people are taking to the streets against the 'dictatorship of the ultra-rich', asserting that the labor movement can defeat President Trump.

Researchers tracking US strikes report that after increases in 2022 and 2023, strike activity dropped significantly in 2024 and remained low in 2025, according to Jacobin.

More than 100,000 students across the United States walked out of classrooms in the largest one-day student strike in over 80 years. The action was joined by Sunrise Movement and community mobilizations nationwide.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has disrupted the Republican Party's hopes for a favorable midterm map. Georgia, once reliably Republican, is now a swing state with two Democratic senators and a Republican governor.

Critics argue that the US Supreme Court's supermajority ruling on the Voting Rights Act effectively pretends racism no longer exists. The decision is seen as disregarding the history of racial discrimination.

A ProPublica investigation found that at least 75 career officials who helped prevent Donald Trump's attempt to overturn the 2020 election have been removed or left their positions. The institutional guardrails that held are now weakened.

CounterPunch reported that it surpassed 2.7 million readers in 2025, a milestone attributed to steady growth since its founding about 25 years ago. The outlet noted that only 6,663 readers financially supported the site.

CounterPunch reported that as of 2025, coal is no longer the dominant energy source worldwide, edged out by renewables driven by China's solar boom. However, the world saw a record increase in energy demand.

▸ sentez / synthesis
Bir CounterPunch görüş yazısı, eski Başkan Donald Trump'ın Beyaz Saray'da 400 milyon dolarlık bir balo salonu inşa etme önerisini eleştiriyor ve bunu Reichskanzlei'ye benzetiyor. Yazar, Trump'ın dans etmesinin motivasyon olduğu fikrini reddediyor.
A CounterPunch opinion piece criticizes former President Donald Trump's proposal to build a $400 million ballroom at the White House, comparing it to a Reichskanzlei. The author dismisses the idea that Trump's dancing is the motivation.
Yorum, Demokrat ünlülerin Trump tehdidini hafife aldığını söylüyor
Commentary says Democratic celebrities underestimate Trump threat

CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis

A CounterPunch opinion piece argues that wealthy Democratic celebrities like Bette Midler and Bruce Springsteen, while opposing Trump, fail to fully grasp the danger of his fascist regime. The piece criticizes their stance as insufficient.

Israel seizes Gaza flotilla vessels in international waters

▸ sentez / synthesis
CounterPunch, 30 Nisan 2026'da İsrail güçlerinin Girit açıklarında uluslararası sularda Global Sumud Filosu'na ait yirmiden fazla gemiyi durdurup ele geçirdiğini bildiriyor. Operasyonda silahlı komandolar, insansız hava araçları ve elektronik karıştırma kullanıldı.
CounterPunch reports that on April 30, 2026, Israeli forces intercepted and seized over twenty vessels of the Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters near Crete. The operation involved armed commandos, drones, and electronic jamming.

CounterPunch published an article disputing white nationalist claims about Latino migration to the US, arguing that loose definitions and manipulated statistics misrepresent the situation. The piece references Trump's promise to close the border.

CounterPunch reports that Jerome Powell likely has his last meeting as Fed Chair this week, with Kevin Warsh expected to be approved as successor by June. The article provides an appraisal of Powell's tenure.

A CounterPunch article criticizes anti-war activists who, while condemning US-Israeli aggression on Iran, qualify their position by mentioning Iranian killings. The author argues this undermines the anti-war message.

CounterPunch reports on A.M. Gittlitz's book 'Metropolitans: New York Baseball, Class Struggle, and the People's Team', which highlights three phases of the Mets franchise history from 1776 to 2005.

▸ sentez / synthesis

The Irish farmer protests are examined as a case study in how rightwing populism has gripped rural areas, particularly farmers. The Iran war is creating challenges for key constituencies in Trump's base.

Democratic National Committee chair Ken Martin faces controversy for breaking his promise to release the party's autopsy report on the 2024 election. The decision not to publicly scrutinize what went wrong remains disputed.

An essay contends that society crushes ideas by refusing to think or change, rather than by suppressing them. It quotes a character from Ursula K. Le Guin's novel.

An essay argues that the world faces multiple crises, including a supply chain crisis due to the Hormuz Strait shutdown, and calls for declaring interdependence with each other.

▸ sentez / synthesis

CounterPunch discusses the progress of Black political representation in the United States since the Voting Rights Act of 1965, noting that African Americans held less than 2 percent of seats despite being over 10 percent of the population.

An essay contends that the greatest threat to America is not government overreach but the suppression of speech, arguing that even Thomas Jefferson would be on a watch list today.

CounterPunch reports that Donald Trump's re-election reignited a feud between progressive and centrist Democrats. Progressives urge the party to rally around universal health care and raising wages, arguing such populist measures are winning issues.

Common Dreams published a commentary criticizing a right-wing Supreme Court ruling that gutted the Voting Rights Act, stating it ensures communities of color will increasingly be denied a voice in their own destiny.

AlterNet reports on a study suggesting that believing in MAGA conspiracy theories, such as opposing COVID-19 vaccines or claiming the 2020 election was stolen, could negatively affect one's dating life.

AlterNet reports that a news anchor confronted Senator Rick Scott (R-Fla.) over his sudden support for removing the Senate filibuster, despite opposing similar moves when Democrats were in the majority.

AlterNet reports that economist Scott Lincicome said the Supreme Court ruling dismantling Trump's tariff was a victory for the rule of law, but voters are unlikely to get reimbursed for costs incurred under the illegal taxes.

AlterNet reports that President Trump is targeting Indiana Republicans who did not vote for gerrymandering, but faces opposition from members of his own party, including state Senate leader Rodric Bray.

BreakThrough News reports live from Chicago, where students, teachers, and workers are mobilizing as part of a total shutdown with the slogan 'No Work. No School. No Shopping.' on May Day.

Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel presided over a meeting with 80 workers from strategic sectors ahead of International Workers' Day. A new documentary featuring his first extensive interview with a US journalist is set to premiere on May 14.

Maryland activists halt ICE detention center plan

▸ sentez / synthesis
Maryland'de toplum aktivistleri, bir deponun ICE gözaltı merkezine dönüştürülmesi planlarını durdurdu. Proje, Trump'ın One Big Beautiful Bill kapsamında ayrılan 45 milyar dolarlık fonun bir parçasıydı.
In Maryland, community activists forced a pause on plans to convert a warehouse into an ICE detention center. The project was part of a $45 billion allocation under Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill.
Trump yönetimi sınır duvarı için antik alanı buldozerle yıktı
Trump administration bulldozed ancient site for border wall
1 src · 1 it2026-04-30 23:01
The Intercept
▸ sentez / synthesis
The Intercept'e göre Trump yönetimi, ikinci bir sınır duvarına yer açmak için 1000 yıllık bir arkeolojik alanı buldozerle yıktı. İç Güvenlik Bakanlığı, alanın bulunduğu yaban hayatı sığınağıyla korunması için görüşmeler yapıyordu.
The Trump administration bulldozed a 1,000-year-old archaeological site to make way for a second border wall, according to The Intercept. The Department of Homeland Security had been in talks with the wildlife refuge hosting the site to ensure its protection.
Analistler, kaotik davranışlar nedeniyle Trump'ın zihinsel uygunluğunu sorguluyor
Analysts question Trump's mental fitness amid chaotic behavior

AlterNet · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Emekli Deniz Harp Okulu profesörü Tom Nichols ve diğer analistler, Başkan Trump'ın bilişsel durumuyla ilgili endişelerini dile getirerek 'saçmalık yağmuru' ve Yüksek Mahkeme'nin Oy Hakkı Yasası kararından habersiz görünmesini örnek gösterdi. Davranışlarının bilişsel gerilemeye işaret ettiğini savunuyorlar.
Retired Naval War College professor Tom Nichols and other analysts have raised concerns about President Trump's cognitive state, citing a 'firehose of lunacy' and his apparent unawareness of a Supreme Court ruling on the Voting Rights Act. They argue his behavior indicates cognitive decline.
Nijerli aktivistler Fransız şirketlerinin sınır dışı edilmesini ve CFA frangının kaldırılmasını talep ediyor
Nigerien activists demand expulsion of French firms and CFA franc abolition

▸ sentez / synthesis
Nijer'deki öğrenciler ve aktivistler, Fransız şirketlerinin Sahel'den sınır dışı edilmesini ve CFA frangının kaldırılmasını talep ediyor. Sahel Devletleri İttifakı'nı ortak bir para biriminin oluşturulmasını hızlandırmaya çağırıyorlar.
Students and activists in Niger are demanding the expulsion of French corporations from the Sahel and the abolition of the CFA franc. They are calling on the Alliance of Sahel States to accelerate the creation of a common currency.

▸ sentez / synthesis
Trump dönemi İç Güvenlik eski genelkurmay başkanı Miles Taylor, başkanın İran'la savaş planlarının 'çözüldüğünü' ve gerçeklik için fazla haince olduğunu söyledi. Yorumlarını MSNBC'de yaptı.
Miles Taylor, former Homeland Security chief of staff under Trump, said the president's plans for war with Iran are 'unraveled' and too treacherous for reality. He made the comments on MSNBC.

Criticism of media focus on Hasan Piker amid Gaza war coverage
3 src · 3 it2026-04-30 21:56
The Real News Network · Electronic Intifada · Jacobin
▸ sentez / synthesis
Birçok yayın organı, ana akım medyayı Twitch yayıncısı Hasan Piker'ın yorumlarına odaklanırken Hind Rajab vakası da dahil olmak üzere İsrail'in Filistinlilere yönelik öldürmelerini görmezden gelmekle eleştirdi. Eleştirmenlere göre The Atlantic, Piker hakkında birden fazla makale yayınlarken Gazze savaşı hakkında hiçbir makale yayınlamadı.
Several outlets criticized mainstream media for focusing on Twitch streamer Hasan Piker's comments while ignoring Israeli killings of Palestinians, including the case of Hind Rajab. The Atlantic published multiple articles on Piker but none on the Gaza war, according to critics.
Trump, Yüksek Mahkeme'nin oy hakkı kararının ardından seçim hilesini teşvik etti
Trump urges gerrymandering after Supreme Court voting rights ruling
2 src · 2 it2026-04-30 21:52
Asia Times · AlterNet
▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Trump, Oy Hakkı Yasası'nı zayıflatan Yüksek Mahkeme kararından yararlanarak bir valiyi seçim hilesi yapmaya teşvik etti ve bir başkasını bir ön seçimi askıya aldığı için övdü. Analistler, kararın 2026 ara seçimlerinde Demokrat katılımını bastırabileceği uyarısında bulunuyor.
President Trump moved to capitalize on a Supreme Court decision weakening the Voting Rights Act by urging a governor to gerrymander and praising another for suspending a primary. Analysts warn the ruling could suppress Democratic turnout in the 2026 midterms.
Warren, Hegseth'in askeri hisse senedi ticareti konusunda kendisiyle çeliştiğini yakaladı
Warren catches Hegseth contradicting himself on military stock trading
1 src · 1 it2026-04-30 21:10
Truthout
▸ sentez / synthesis
Senatör Elizabeth Warren, Savunma Bakanı Pete Hegseth'i kongre oturumlarının ikinci gününde iddia edilen askeri hisse senedi ticareti konusunda kendisiyle çelişirken yakaladı. Bir protestocu da oturumu bölerek Hegseth'i savaş suçlusu olarak nitelendirdi.
Senator Elizabeth Warren caught Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth contradicting himself regarding alleged military stock trading during his second day of congressional hearings. A protester also interrupted the hearing, calling Hegseth a war criminal.

A report by Esquire columnist Charles P. Pierce notes a record number of Republican retirements from the House and Senate, with many candidates dropping out. The article suggests the reasons are obvious.

Law professor Richard Hasen denounced Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito as a coward after Alito authored the Louisiana v. Callais decision, which weakens the Voting Rights Act. Hasen accused Alito of lying to himself or the public.

The Intercept · Common Dreams
▸ sentez / synthesis
Senatör Ron Wyden, gözetim yetkisinin sürekli ihlallerini ortaya çıkaran gizli bir Yabancı İstihbarat Gözetim Mahkemesi görüşünün gizliliğinin kaldırılması için baskı yapıyor. Wyden, gizliliğin kaldırılması karşılığında FISA'nın kısa süreli uzatılmasını önerdi ve sonuçlar konusunda uyarıda bulunan Senatör Tom Cotton da dahil olmak üzere NSA destekçilerinin eleştirilerini çekti.
Senator Ron Wyden is pushing to declassify a secret Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court opinion that revealed continuing violations of surveillance authority. Wyden proposed a short-term extension of FISA in exchange for declassification, drawing criticism from NSA supporters including Senator Tom Cotton, who warned of consequences.

▸ sentez / synthesis

Olay ayrıca, Londra'da Yahudi erkeklere yönelik bıçaklı saldırının ardından Başbakan Keir Starmer'ı antisemitizme tepkisi nedeniyle yuhalayan İngiliz protestocuların eleştirilerini çekti.
Israel seized a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters near Greece, detaining 175 activists. The UN's top Palestine expert condemned the action as piracy. The incident also drew criticism from UK protesters who heckled Prime Minister Keir Starmer over his response to antisemitism following a knife attack on Jewish men in London.

The Supreme Court struck down Louisiana's voting map designed to create a second majority-Black district. A civil rights lawyer called the ruling a 'free pass to discriminate.'

AlterNet reports that voters who supported Donald Trump in 2024 due to economic concerns are increasingly frustrated as inflation persists. The article cites i Paper reporter Kieron Monks.

AlterNet reports that the U.S. Coast Guard will run out of money at the end of the week if Congress does not act. The shutdown, now 75 days long, is the longest in U.S. history and affects the Department of Homeland Security.

President Donald Trump is suing the IRS for $10 billion, roughly two-thirds of the agency's annual budget. A judge has caused the case to hit a snag due to a conflict of interest, as a sitting president cannot pocket money from the government he controls.

War Secretary Pete Hegseth testified to Congress on Wednesday, boasting of a deadlier war machine while brushing off queries about civilian harm. The Intercept reports that the US is unleashing 'devastating civilian harm globally.'

New York Supreme Court Justice David Fried rejected a claim by US Rep. Mike Lawler (R-NY) to oust Democratic candidate Effie Phillips-Staley's petitions to be on the ballot. Lawler is the only Republican incumbent in New York whose district is considered competitive.

Israel intensified assaults on Palestinians in Gaza and the West Bank concurrently with its escalation in Lebanon. In southern Lebanon, an Israeli strike killed 4-year-old Mila Zayyat and her pregnant mother Ruqaya Zidan.

House Speaker Mike Johnson added a cryptocurrency provision unrelated to domestic spying to secure support from the Freedom Caucus for a domestic surveillance law. The provision is unlikely to pass the Senate.

US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth faced criticism from lawmakers for his handling of the Iran war, including allegations of war crimes for a 'no quarter' order. Hegseth dismissed concerns about troop deaths as 'consequence of conflict.' Iran is reportedly setting terms for ending the war.

Minneapolis activists are accusing a local House candidate of 'cosplaying' as a protester, blurring the line between solidarity and opportunism. The Intercept reports concerns that Democrats are trying to co-opt movements for votes.

California may soon have over 300 data centers, with one proposed facility requiring 750,000 gallons of water per day. Locals worry about threats to water supply.

A federal appeals court declined to rehear President Trump's appeal in the $83 million E. Jean Carroll civil defamation case. The decision allows Trump to petition the US Supreme Court on presidential immunity claims.
Yüksek Mahkeme kararı Oy Hakkı Yasası'nı zayıflatıyor, eleştirmenler seçim hilesi uyarısı yapıyor
Supreme Court ruling weakens Voting Rights Act, critics warn of gerrymandering

▸ sentez / synthesis
The Real News Network ve Common Dreams, ABD Yüksek Mahkemesi'nin Louisiana'da ikinci bir çoğunluğu siyahi bölgeyi içeren bir kongre haritasını iptal ederek Oy Hakkı Yasası'nın 2. Bölümünü zayıflattığını bildirdi. Eleştirmenler, kararın Cumhuriyetçilerin seçim hilesini güçlendirdiğini söylüyor.
The Real News Network and Common Dreams report that the US Supreme Court struck down a Louisiana congressional map with a second majority-Black district, gutting Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act. Critics say the ruling empowers Republican gerrymandering.

Analysis says Trump's ballroom focus masks humiliating strategic defeat

Krugman, Trump'ın balo salonu projesine odaklanmasının İran konusunda aşağılayıcı bir stratejik yenilgiyi maskelediğini söylüyor.
President Trump insists polls showing low approval ratings are fake news, but economist Paul Krugman argues they reflect widespread dissatisfaction. Krugman says Trump's focus on a ballroom project masks a humiliating strategic defeat regarding Iran.

The New York Times reported that the Trump-era DOJ is targeting over 300 U.S. citizens for denaturalization. The New Republic's Matt Ford described the push as 'concerning' and legally shaky.

Michigan'da Filistin yanlısı aktivistler Demokratik aday belirleme kurultayında zaferler elde etti.
In Pennsylvania, Chris Rabb's stance on Palestine has resonated with voters in a Democratic primary. Meanwhile, mainstream media is increasingly reflecting public outrage over Israeli actions, and Francesca Albanese criticized India's ties with Israel. In Michigan, pro-Palestinian activists achieved victories at the Democratic nominating convention.

A federal judge dismissed a Justice Department lawsuit that sought access to Arizona's detailed voter registration records. The ruling is seen as a setback for President Trump's efforts to restrict voting access.

Investigative journalist Carol D. Leonnig reported that acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told his lieutenants he planned a 'perp-walk' for James Comey before charging him, according to anonymous DOJ sources.

A GOP strategist said the sour economy has undermined President Trump's messaging, as the White House focuses on building a $400 million ballroom. A poll shows 57% of Americans believe the country is on the wrong track.

Communities across the U.S. are resisting the construction of AI data centers, which tech companies are pushing to dominate AI infrastructure. Critics say the data centers prioritize corporate profits over community needs.

The Grayzone reports that Israel created nine English-language websites to manipulate AI platforms into denying established accounts of Hind Rajab's killing. The campaign was contracted through Trump's former campaign manager.

A scholar stated that President Trump's war in Iran is one of the most unpopular in modern American history, and many Republicans feel trapped despite opposition from their own base.

Former FBI agent Michael Feinberg and a former CIA director criticized acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's indictment of former CIA head James Comey, calling it thin and lacking decency. The indictment allegedly involved threats with seashells.

Senator Lindsey Graham and other Trump allies are pushing a bill through Congress that would allocate $400 million in taxpayer funds to rebuild Trump's ballroom, with $332 million covered by taxpayers.

The Pentagon provided musician Kid Rock with a helicopter ride to help him film a tour promotion. Three days before the flight, Kid Rock had blamed poor ticket sales on being 'suppressed' for his views.

May 1 is a crucial deadline under the War Powers Act for President Trump's military actions in Iran. Trump can seek a 30-day extension but must show Congress that the war is an 'unavoidable military necessity.'

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's testimony in his criminal trial was cancelled again due to security concerns. President Donald Trump had called for Netanyahu to be pardoned in a request to Israel's president last year.

The US and Philippines launched their largest ever Balikatan military exercises with 17,000 troops from multiple countries. American missile systems were deployed to the Luzon Strait, about 100 miles south of Taiwan.

Studies suggest that delaying hepatitis B vaccinations could result in hundreds of infant infections. Critics say the CDC's most recent vaccine recommendations are not based on science.

The Trump administration's Justice Department is moving to make it easier to deport DACA recipients. Representative Delia Ramirez accused the administration of trying to 'weaponize the court system' against immigrants.

Hundreds of educators across North Carolina are joining a 'no work, no school, no shopping' national shutdown on May 1 to demand more funding for schools. The action is led by the Asheville City Association of Educators.

Far-right Israeli settlers are increasingly crossing the border into Syria, with at least tacit support from the Israeli military, as part of a push for 'Greater Israel.'

Apollo Global Management, backed by a billionaire, bankrupted two of Maine's largest paper mills, eliminating over 1,000 jobs. The same billionaire is now funding a PAC to keep Senator Susan Collins in office.

Multiple reports highlight ongoing Israeli military operations in Gaza and Lebanon, including alleged war crimes. The UK Foreign Office closed its unit tracking Israeli violations, while Palestinian Nakba commemorations were blocked by Israeli police. The United Arab Emirates announced its departure from OPEC, a major blow to the oil cartel. Meanwhile, the war on Iran drove oil prices higher, contributing to BP reporting £2.4 billion in profits for the first quarter of 2026.

An incarcerated individual in New York stated that he would refuse medical treatment and choose death unless the state changes its parole laws, highlighting the harsh conditions of prolonged incarceration.

AlterNet reports that a White House leak reveals internal fears that the war with Iran could become a costly quagmire, harming the US economy and President Trump politically. Economists warn that higher oil prices will hurt consumers.

AlterNet published an article alleging that $54.6 billion in secret Pentagon spending is funding surveillance and warfare infrastructure that could be used against Americans, including drone technology.

The Intercept argues that integrating prediction market betting into journalism harms reporting and democracy. The piece calls for an end to such practices before they become entrenched.

ProPublica reports that a man died in a Florida jail, and experts say the private company running the facility should have sent him to the hospital. The company's failure to provide timely medical care is under scrutiny.

CounterPunch reports an unprecedented shift of resources from domestic investments in the United States to the military-industrial complex, comparable only to the buildup to World War II.

CounterPunch reports on an assassination attempt at the Washington Hilton, drawing comparisons to the 1981 shooting of Ronald Reagan by John Hinckley Jr. The article notes that the Hinckley shooting led to the Brady Bill and background checks.

CounterPunch reports that Israeli law criminalizes speech that 'minimalizes' the events of October 7th or challenges the government's account, under Article 24 of the Anti-Terrorism Law, with penalties of up to five years imprisonment for phrases like 'from the river to the sea.'

CounterPunch publishes a personal account of a family in communist Prague, where the father was repeatedly arrested and tortured for dissident activities, and the mother made small concessions to authorities.

President Donald Trump is using a recent security breach at the White House to push for construction of a $400 million ballroom, citing national security. Senate Majority Leader John Thune has been noncommittal on the proposal. Common Dreams and AlterNet report on the controversy.

Wall Street Journal yazarı Cumhuriyetçileri Gizli Servis'e saldırmamaları konusunda uyarırken, The Intercept bu tür girişimlerin ardındaki toplumsal faktörleri analiz etti.
An apparent assassination attempt on President Donald Trump on Saturday has drawn criticism of the Secret Service from some conservatives, while others suspect the event was staged. A Wall Street Journal columnist warned Republicans against attacking the Secret Service, and The Intercept analyzed the societal factors behind such attempts.

Conservative scholars warn that President Trump's Cabinet picks risk violating the Constitution, and that his leadership style resembles that of Julius Caesar. A loophole may allow loyalist Todd Blanche to remain at the DOJ without Senate confirmation.

A defense expert stated that the proposed Trump-class battleships prioritize the president's ego. The firing of Naval Secretary John Phelan may be linked to difficulties in delivering on Trump's demand for the new battleships.

Gaza's Deir Al-Balah held its first election since 2005. The vote was lauded as a step forward but occurred amid ongoing genocide and occupation, and a recent law limiting candidate eligibility.

The builder of a Trump ballroom was awarded $17 million for a project that the Biden administration estimated would cost $3 million. The original cost estimator said the estimate was inflated.

Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who as a senator helped a diplomat with Havana Syndrome, is now accused of being 'MIA' on the issue. The diplomat, Mark Lenzi, said he was targeted after blowing the whistle.

Over half of planned data center projects for 2026 have been delayed or cancelled due to mass organized pushback from communities across the US. Residents are blocking approvals and passing referendums against AI infrastructure.

President Trump called for ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel over a joke made days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting. The joke was likely a reference to the president's declining health, not a call for violence.

Jacobin argues that after the failed Arab revolutions, Egypt's elite under Abdel el-Sisi implemented a violent counterrevolution that consolidated a new form of state terror.

After a shooting outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner, President Trump is using the incident to argue for expanding surveillance powers under Section 702 of FISA, which faces a deadline. Reports from Truthout, Asia Times, and The Real News Network cover the story.

According to Truthout, Iran's religious minority communities are overlooked victims of the US-Israeli war on Iran, despite claims of fighting for their rights.

The Ramones' self-titled debut album celebrates its 50th anniversary. The record is described as speaking for those forgotten by the system, serving as a blueprint for later political punk.

The abortion rights movement has secured several key wins, demonstrating that it is possible to reclaim the narrative and ensure access for those who have been denied for too long, according to Truthout.

A report from the Center for Economic and Policy Research (CEPR) finds that the expansion of US sanctions on Cuba since 2017 likely caused a sharp increase in Cuba's infant mortality rate, according to Common Dreams.

Long Island'da Vibrio adlı et yiyen bir bakteri tespit edildi ve sağlık uyarıları yapıldı. Belirtiler arasında mide bulantısı, ishal ve ciddi vakalarda ölüm yer alıyor.
A flesh-eating bacteria called Vibrio has been found on Long Island, prompting health warnings. Symptoms include nausea, diarrhea, and in severe cases, death.

Iranian Group Files War Crimes Evidence Against US and Israel at ICC

Ayrı ayrı raporlar, Gazze'deki tarım arazilerinin çoğunun İsrail bombardımanı ve müsaderesi nedeniyle kullanılamaz hale geldiğini belirtiyor.
An Iranian group has submitted evidence to the International Criminal Court alleging that US-Israeli strikes destroyed civilian infrastructure in Gaza, including homes, hospitals, and universities. Separately, reports indicate that most farmland in Gaza is unusable due to Israeli bombing and seizure.

New Mexico has enacted a law ending state and local cooperation with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Activists say such measures are key to obstructing the Trump administration's mass deportation efforts.

The U.S. Senate voted on April 16 to overturn a 2023 ban on mining in 225,000 acres of protected federal land near Minnesota's Boundary Waters. The area is vital for the region's ecosystem and climate resilience.

Declassified documents confirm for the first time that the CIA carried out MK-ULTRA experiments on North Korean prisoners of war in U.S. custody. The documents also reveal plans for more invasive experimentation.

BreakThrough News reports that the United States has tightened its fuel blockade on Cuba, but the island refuses to surrender. Manolo de los Santos, who recently returned from Havana, provides an on-the-ground report on how Cubans are surviving and resisting.

A Harvard Youth poll found that only 15% of Americans aged 18-29 trust the government, and 33% trust the upcoming midterm elections will be fair. Many suspect President Trump will derail the election.

President Trump told reporters he was never much in favor of prediction markets, calling the concept a casino. His comments came as prosecutors close in on suspects in insider trading cases.

FBI Director Kash Patel was arrested twice in his youth for alcohol-related incidents, including public urination. Patel said the incidents were not representative of his usual conduct.

Twenty donors to the Southern Poverty Law Center said the alleged fraud being prosecuted by the Trump administration was exactly how they hoped the group would spend their money. The donors rejected the DOJ's claims.

The Federal Communications Commission is updating its ratings system to include warnings for transgender content. The sole Democratic FCC commissioner called the move 'a solution in search of a problem.'

On March 15, activists led by Wayne Hsiung rescued 22 beagles from Ridglan Farms in Wisconsin. Twenty-seven people were arrested, and Hsiung vowed to return, as reported by CounterPunch.

2 src · 3 it2026-04-24 05:41

▸ sentez / synthesis

Beyaz Saray bütçe şefi İran savaşı için maliyet tahmini vermeyi reddetti; uzmanlar maliyetin 1 trilyon doları aşabileceğini belirtiyor.
Iran announced the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after a temporary ceasefire, but President Trump said the US naval blockade remains until a transaction is complete. The White House budget chief refused to provide cost estimates for the Iran war, which experts say could exceed $1 trillion.

President Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that Operation Epic Fury has destroyed Iran's military capacity, including its navy, air force, and leadership. The claims were made after Trump declared a ceasefire with Iran.

MSNBC anchor Nicole Wallace criticized President Trump, noting that many MAGA influencers and podcasters are abandoning him as his popularity declines. She said they knew Trump's negative traits all along.

Immigration attorney Nicholas Berning reported that his office is flooded with calls from Americans considering moving to Canada due to President Trump's policies. A new Canadian policy is attracting many.

The Milwaukee Journal Sentinel reports that voters who helped Trump win Wisconsin in 2024 are now abandoning him. A pool of Trump voters in the state has significantly shrunk.

Iran refuses negotiations with US citing mistrust and threats

MintPress News
▸ sentez / synthesis
MintPress News, Washington tehditlerden müzakerelere geçerken İran'ın artık görüşmeleri güvenilir bulmadığının sinyalini verdiğini bildiriyor. Dr. Seyed Mohammad Marandi, Tahran'ın ABD'nin yakınlaşma girişimlerini neden reddettiğini, artan saldırılar, derin güvensizlik ve müzakere ekibine yönelik suikast tehditlerini gerekçe göstererek açıklıyor.
MintPress News reports that as Washington shifts from threats to negotiations, Iran is signaling it no longer sees talks as credible. Dr. Seyed Mohammad Marandi explains why Tehran is rejecting U.S. outreach, citing escalating attacks, deep mistrust, and assassination threats against its negotiating team.

▸ sentez / synthesis
Meksika Devlet Başkanı Claudia Sheinbaum, CIA ajanlarının kuzeydeki Chihuahua eyaletinde yetkisiz olarak faaliyet gösterdiğini doğruladı. Meksika hükümetiyle koordine edilmeyen bir uyuşturucuyla mücadele operasyonunun ardından iki ajan bir trafik kazasında öldü.
Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum confirmed that CIA agents were operating in the northern state of Chihuahua without authorization. Two agents were killed in a car accident following a counter-narcotics operation that was not coordinated with the Mexican government.

Former Obama and Biden UN appointee Andrew Weinstein said President Trump's attacks on the media have become so dangerous that journalists face a choice: 'be quiet or be targeted.' He lamented the adversarial relationship with the press.

Başkan Donald Trump, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'ı destekleyen sağlık bilincine sahip kadın ağırlıklı MAHA koalisyonunun desteğini kaybediyor. Trump'ın tehlikeli maddelerin yasaklanması gibi konulardaki tutum değişikliği hayal kırıklığına yol açtı.
President Donald Trump is losing support from the Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) coalition, a health-conscious female group that backed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. before he endorsed Trump. Disillusionment grew after Trump reversed positions on issues such as banning dangerous substances.

Lara Trump faced criticism on social media after claiming that President Donald Trump's life would be easier if he were not president. Critics pointed out that Trump's wealth has increased significantly since taking office.

A poll found that only 4 percent of Democrats support increasing military aid to Israel. Separate polling showed Congress's disapproval ratings tied an all-time high of 86 percent.

An Arizona state senator who attended the January 6 attack proposed a bill to change the legal definition of a riot, allowing prison sentences for protesters who damage property.

A Trump envoy suggested that Italy should replace Iran in the World Cup. Iran has qualified for the tournament and intends to send its players.

Footage has emerged of the January 2022 arrest of Zach Witkoff, son of US Special Envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff, for drug possession at a Miami nightclub. During the arrest, officers told him to stop dropping names.

More Republicans are urging President Donald Trump to stop his legal attacks on Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, noting that Trump will appoint Powell's replacement when his term expires next month. Critics say the attacks are pointless and damaging.

Bu arada aktivistler, ICE ile bağları nedeniyle Citizens Bank'ı protesto etti ve Trump, göçmenlere saldıran ırkçı bir podcast paylaştı. Eleştirmenler, yönetimin göçmenleri hedef almak için bürokratik önlemler kullandığını söylüyor.
Representative Rashida Tlaib introduced a bill to ban ICE from using warehouses to detain immigrants. Meanwhile, activists protested Citizens Bank for its ties to ICE, and Trump shared a racist podcast attacking immigrants. Critics say the administration is using bureaucratic measures to target immigrants.
Güney Kore'de on binlerce Samsung işçisi daha yüksek ücret için miting düzenledi
Tens of thousands of Samsung workers rally for higher wages in South Korea
1 src · 1 it2026-04-23 19:28
BreakThrough News
▸ sentez / synthesis
Güney Kore'nin Pyeongtaek kentinde yaklaşık 40.000 sendikalı Samsung işçisi Perşembe günü daha yüksek ücret ve ikramiye talebiyle miting düzenledi. Sendika, kârın daha adil dağıtılmasını talep ediyor ve 21 Mayıs'ta grev yapma tehdidinde bulunuyor. Samsung yakın zamanda 38,5 milyar doları aşan kâr açıkladı.
About 40,000 unionized Samsung workers in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, rallied on Thursday demanding higher wages and bonuses. The union is calling for better profit distribution and has threatened a strike on May 21. Samsung recently reported profits exceeding $38.5 billion.

Navy Secretary John Phelan was abruptly removed from his post, with reports indicating he was fired by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for refusing to disobey a judge's order. Acting Secretary Hung Cao, known for anti-woke stances, has taken over.

Zeteo editor Martin Pengelly suggested the word 'omnishambles', popularized by the TV show 'The Thick of It', to describe the depth of crisis in the Trump administration. The term refers to a situation that is a complete shambles.

A journalism professor suggested that the White House press corps should fact-check President Donald Trump's statements in real time every time he asserts a fact. The proposal aims to counter Trump's frequent falsehoods without being accused of bias.

Researchers argue that work requirements for SNAP benefits do not reduce unemployment, citing hunger and job scarcity as key factors.

Raporda, Trump'ın Küba'ya yönelik saldırılarına ABD'de destek olmadığı, bunun yalnızca aşırı sağcı Küba kökenli Amerikalıların bir azınlığı tarafından yönlendirildiği iddia ediliyor.
According to BreakThrough News, 96,000 people in Cuba are waiting for surgery due to fuel shortages caused by Trump's energy blockade. The report claims there is no support in the US for Trump's attacks on Cuba, driven only by a minority of far-right Cuban Americans.

Activists protested outside Capital One, demanding the bank stop extending credit to Elbit Systems, Israel's largest weapons manufacturer. The protest is part of a campaign to pressure financial institutions to cut ties with companies involved in the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil, a reporter for al-Akhbar, was killed in an Israeli drone strike on Wednesday in al-Baisariyah. Her colleague was also targeted with three strikes, including one on an ambulance. A funeral procession was held on Thursday.

Adam Hanieh warns that a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could lead to a global food catastrophe, amid the worst debt crisis on record for developing countries. The strait is a critical chokepoint for food and energy shipments.

The Real News Network reports that 3,800 meatpacking workers in UFCW Local 7 in Greeley, Colorado launched the industry's first major strike in 40 years. After three weeks, JBS conceded to the workers.

The Real News Network reports that one Palestinian American researcher warned that Israel is seeking annexation without legal burden by advancing its 'Yellow Line' in Gaza.

Senate Republicans blocked an amendment proposed by Senator Bernie Sanders that would have required Americans to pay prices for medications similar to those in other wealthy nations. The amendment aimed to reduce drug prices by half.

Common Dreams reports that Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders voted to accept Paramount Skydance's $110 billion bid to acquire the company. The merger would create a media colossus with CBS, CNN, HBO, and others, but the vote is not the final word on the deal.

Hamle, sektör içeriden kişilere fayda sağlamak olarak görülüyor.
Common Dreams reports that a recent announcement by the U.S. Department of Agriculture to drastically resize and restructure the U.S. Forest Service would effectively eviscerate key research and protections for public lands, according to a new report. The move is seen as benefiting industry insiders.

Birçok opioid mağduru Purdue anlaşmasından mahrum kalacak; ProPublica iletişim arıyor
Many opioid victims will be shut out of Purdue settlement; ProPublica seeks contact
1 src · 2 it2026-04-23 09:00
ProPublica · ProPublica
▸ sentez / synthesis
ProPublica, yıllarca bekledikten sonra birçok opioid mağdurunun Purdue anlaşmasından mahrum kalacağını bildiriyor. ProPublica ayrıca Purdue, Mallinckrodt veya Endo'dan uzlaşma parası bekleyenlerle iletişime geçmek istiyor.
ProPublica reports that after years of waiting, many opioid victims will be shut out of the Purdue settlement. ProPublica is also seeking contact from those waiting for settlement money from Purdue, Mallinckrodt, or Endo.
Trump yönetimi İran politikası ve iç muhalefet nedeniyle eleştiriliyor
Trump administration faces criticism over Iran policy and domestic dissent
2 src · 4 it2026-04-23 08:51
AlterNet · AlterNet · CounterPunch · CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis
Birden fazla kaynak, Trump yönetiminin İran krizini ele alışını ve siyasi muhalefete yönelik baskıyı bildiriyor. Tucker Carlson'ın RNC'de Trump'ı övmesi, Trump'ın İran savaşını sonlandırmakta zorlandığı yönündeki haberlerle tezat oluşturuyor. Yönetim, Filistin yanlısı öğrencilerin vizelerini iptal etti ve göçmenlik yargıçlarını görevden aldı; bu durum muhalefeti susturma suçlamalarına yol açtı.
Multiple outlets report on the Trump administration's handling of the Iran crisis and its crackdown on political dissent. Tucker Carlson's praise of Trump at the RNC contrasts with reports that Trump is struggling to end the war in Iran. The administration has revoked visas of pro-Palestinian students and fired immigration judges, drawing accusations of silencing dissent.
İran krizi, küresel deniz ticareti geçiş noktalarının kırılganlığını vurguluyor
Iran crisis highlights vulnerability of global maritime chokepoints
3 src · 3 it2026-04-23 08:31
Rappler · Asia Times · CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis
İran krizi, Hürmüz Boğazı ve Malakka Boğazı gibi stratejik deniz geçiş noktalarının kırılganlığına dikkat çekti. Analistler, Güney Çin Denizi veya Tayvan Boğazı'nda bir çatışmanın küresel deniz ticaretinin %21'ini aksatabileceği uyarısında bulunuyor. Haberlerde ayrıca Orta Doğu'ya konuşlandırılan ABD savaş gemilerinde gıda sıkıntısı yaşandığı bildiriliyor.
The Iran crisis has drawn attention to the vulnerability of strategic maritime chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz and the Malacca Strait. Analysts warn that a conflict in the South China Sea or Taiwan Strait could disrupt 21% of global maritime trade. Reports also note food shortages on US naval ships deployed to the Middle East.

Brazilian President Lula submitted a bill to Congress on April 14 to reduce the work week to 40 hours, under constitutional urgency requiring a floor vote within 45 days.

CounterPunch examines data showing that people in Republican-controlled states have shorter life expectancies than those in Democratic-controlled states, though causes and mechanisms are debated.

Bazı Cumhuriyetçiler aktif siyasi düşman haline gelirken, anketler onay oranlarında düşüş gösteriyor. Kayıp bir New Jersey kongre üyesi de soru işaretleri yaratıyor.
Multiple reports indicate that President Donald Trump is losing support among former allies and Republican voters. Issues include his handling of Iran, ties to Jeffrey Epstein, and erratic behavior. Some Republicans have become active political enemies, while polls show declining approval. A missing New Jersey congressman has also raised questions.

A New York Times op-ed by military analyst Jennifer Kavanagh argues that blockades are ineffective and that President Trump's blockade of Iran is unlikely to succeed. The piece was highlighted by AlterNet.

Climate activists protested outside the Washington, D.C. offices of Apple, Meta, OpenAI, and Microsoft, demanding a moratorium on hyperscale data centers, which they say consume excessive resources and harm the environment.
ABD, Trump ateşkes uzatması ilan ederken İran yaptırımlarını genişletti
US expands Iran sanctions as Trump declares ceasefire extension
5 src · 8 it2026-04-22 21:06
Truthout · BreakThrough News · Asia Times · Truthout · Truthout · Novara Media · Asia Times · The Intercept
▸ sentez / synthesis
Başkan Donald Trump, ABD-İran çatışmasında ateşkesi uzattı ancak deniz ablukasını sürdürdü ve yaptırımları genişletti. Pentagon değerlendirmesi Trump'ın iddialarıyla çelişirken, bir Cumhuriyetçi senatör ateşkesin durması halinde nükleer bomba kullanılmasını önerdi. Kamuoyu yoklamaları Amerikalıların %70'inin savaşın bir an önce bitmesini istediğini gösteriyor. Pentagon ayrıca yaralı askerleri kayıplar listesinden çıkardı ve bu durum örtbas etme suçlamalarına yol açtı.
President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire in the US-Iran conflict but maintained a naval blockade and expanded sanctions. A Pentagon assessment contradicted Trump's claims, and a GOP senator suggested using a nuclear bomb if ceasefire stalls. Public opinion polls show 70% of Americans want the war to end quickly. The Pentagon also removed wounded troops from casualty lists, drawing accusations of a cover-up.

Democratic lawmakers demanded answers from an official named Patel over drinking allegations and security risks, citing an Atlantic article and Patel's behavior at the Olympics.

Indigenous activists criticize the construction of 103 to 160 massive data centers on Native lands as 'data colonialism' driven by the AI boom.

Israel continues to seize more Gaza territory beyond the so-called Yellow Line, expanding its occupation zone. Hamas demands that Israel implement phase one of the ceasefire, citing over 2,000 violations since October 10, 2025.

A warehouse worker allegedly set fire to a warehouse after demanding a living wage, now facing years in prison. The incident highlights ongoing labor struggles and collective action by workers.

Thirty years after Brazilian military police killed 21 landless workers in the Eldorado do Carajás massacre, most perpetrators remain unpunished, and the struggle for land reform continues.

The Trump administration disclosed a 14-page funding agreement for a $400 million White House ballroom project in response to a FOIA lawsuit by Public Citizen. The agreement involves the White House, National Park Service, and the Trust for the National Mall.

Two US officials who died after a drug lab bust in Mexico were later identified as CIA operatives, contradicting the US ambassador's initial claim that they were embassy staff.

The Trump administration is being sued for approving BP's first project in the Gulf of Mexico since the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster, which killed 11 people and caused the worst oil spill in US history. Truthout reports the lawsuit.

A Pennsylvania court ruled that a state law banning funds for abortion is unconstitutional, as it effectively barred low-income individuals from accessing abortion services through state medical assistance. Truthout reports the decision.
Virginia seçmenleri Trump destekli yeniden bölgelendirme planını reddetti
Virginia voters reject Trump-backed redistricting plan in special election

The Real News Network
▸ sentez / synthesis
Virginia seçmenleri, eleştirmenlerin 2026 ara seçimlerini çalmayı amaçladığını söylediği Trump destekli bir yeniden bölgelendirme planına büyük bir darbe vurdu. Demokrat Vali Abigail Spanberger, seçmenlerin daha fazla Cumhuriyetçi sandalyeye hak kazandığını iddia eden bir başkana karşı geri adım attığını söyledi.
Virginia voters dealt a major blow to a Trump-backed redistricting plan that critics said aimed to steal the 2026 midterms. Democratic Governor Abigail Spanberger said voters pushed back against a president who claims entitlement to more Republican seats.

Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, while U.S. President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran. Iran demands the U.S. lift its naval blockade as a condition for new talks, and Trump says resuming war remains an option.

New research from Frontier Group and Environment America Research indicates that the rapid growth of data centers is delaying the transition to a cleaner energy grid, potentially harming air quality. The report was released for Earth Day.

Germany and Italy blocked a measure to suspend the European Union's trade relationship with Israel. Spanish Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares said 'Europe's credibility is at stake.'

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned of impending global food shortages and rationing as a result of the US-Israeli war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Israel sentenced soldiers for an incident involving a Jesus statue, while dropping charges against soldiers filmed allegedly raping a Palestinian detainee.

Israeli settlers opened fire on a Palestinian school in Al-Mughayyir in the West Bank, killing a 14-year-old student and a 32-year-old man, and injuring several children. Settlers and soldiers had entered the village.

Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez renewed calls to oust President Trump after a report that he was excluded from the Situation Room. She remarked that it might be better if he were on a golf course.

Deaths in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody have surged in 2026, averaging one per week. Advocate Setareh Ghandehari attributes the rise to rampant medical neglect and abuse.

A federal judge overturned the Kennedy Declaration, which had forced 40 hospitals to stop providing gender-affirming care for minors. The ruling blocks the Trump administration's effort to restrict such care.

BreakThrough News reports that the Iranian government is paying for hotel stays for families internally displaced by U.S.-Israeli bombings. A four-star hotel in central Tehran hosts dozens of families, with the municipality covering costs.

Drop Site News reports that fishermen in Ecuador allege they were blindfolded and held hostage for eight days by U.S. forces amid airstrikes and reports of torture under Ecuador's U.S.-backed military regime.

Report: Fossil fuels cost households $12 trillion yearly in hidden costs

▸ sentez / synthesis

Common Dreams reports that a new study by 350.org finds fossil fuels cost households an additional $12 trillion per year in taxpayer handouts, health impacts, and extreme weather damage, equivalent to a $23 million per minute subsidy to Big Oil.

A judge ordered the release of the El Gamal family, including two 5-year-olds, who were held in an ICE detention facility for nearly a year. The family reportedly faced severe medical neglect at the Dilley facility.

Common Dreams reports that the Environmental Working Group filed a federal lawsuit against the EPA for unlawfully delaying a response to its petition seeking stricter limits on glyphosate in oats and a ban on its use as a pre-harvest drying agent.

Jacobin reports that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has choked off helium supplies, a critical resource for medical procedures, research, and technology, causing a global shortage.

▸ sentez / synthesis
Birden fazla kaynak, ABD'nin arabuluculuk yaptığı İran ile ateşkes müzakerelerinin İsrail eylemleri tarafından baltalandığını bildiriyor. DropSite News ve The Real News Network, İsrail'in görüşmeleri sabote ettiğini, Trump yönetiminin ise anlaşmaya bağlı kaldığını detaylandırıyor. Tartışmalar ayrıca Lübnan'ı da içeren daha geniş ABD-İsrail ateşkes matrisini kapsıyor.
Multiple sources report that US-brokered ceasefire negotiations with Iran are being undermined by Israeli actions. DropSite News and The Real News Network detail how Israel is sabotaging the talks, while the Trump administration remains committed to a deal. Discussions also cover the broader US-Israeli ceasefire matrix involving Lebanon.

CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis

CounterPunch argues that Pentagon spending is out of control, contrasting past presidents like Eisenhower and Carter who understood limits on force with more recent administrations that engaged in poorly planned military interventions.

CounterPunch argues that Pentagon spending is out of control, contrasting past presidents who understood limits of force with recent limited planning leading to conflicts.

CounterPunch scrutinizes how the United States became a menace to itself and others, recalling its early vision as a peaceful democracy without entangling alliances.

CounterPunch, rekor borsa fiyatlarını kutlamanın yanlış olduğunu savunuyor ve bunu yüksek mısır fiyatlarını kutlamaya benzetiyor ve hissedarların nüfusun sınırlı bir kısmı olduğunu belirtiyor.
CounterPunch argues that celebrating record high stock prices is misguided, comparing it to celebrating high corn prices, and notes that shareholders are a limited share of the population.

CounterPunch reports on Supreme Court immigration case

CounterPunch reports on a Supreme Court immigration case, describing the author's experience of being arrested for praying on the court steps during anti-poverty work.

Senate votes on blocking bomb transfer to Israel

CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis
ABD Senatosu, Senatör Bernie Sanders öncülüğünde İsrail'e 1.000 kiloluk bomba ve askeri teçhizat transferini engellemek için bir karar tasarısını oyladı. Oylama, ABD solundaki kesimler tarafından yakından izlendi.
The U.S. Senate voted on a resolution led by Senator Bernie Sanders to block the transfer of 1,000-pound bombs and military equipment to Israel. The vote was closely watched by the U.S. left.
İran'ın 10 maddelik planı müzakereler için temel olarak görülüyor
Iran's 10-point plan seen as basis for negotiations
1 src · 1 it2026-04-21 05:50
CounterPunch
▸ sentez / synthesis
CounterPunch'a göre, Donald Trump yönetimindeki ABD, İran'la samimiyetsiz müzakereleri saldırılara kılıf olarak kullandı ve bunu tekrar deniyor. 8 Nisan'da iki haftalık bir ateşkes başladı ve İran'ın 10 maddelik planı hâlâ müzakereler için uygulanabilir bir temel olarak görülüyor.
CounterPunch reports that the U.S. under Donald Trump has used disingenuous negotiations with Iran to cover for attacks, and is attempting to do so again. A two-week ceasefire began on April 8, and Iran's 10-point plan is still considered a workable basis for talks.

China tanker defies U.S. blockade in Strait of Hormuz
2 src · 2 it2026-04-21 05:50
CounterPunch · Brasil de Fato
▸ sentez / synthesis
Çin tankeri Rich Starry, 14 Nisan'da Hürmüz Boğazı'nı geçerek ABD ablukasına meydan okudu. Brasil de Fato'ya konuşan bir siyaset bilimci, ABD'nin kırmızı çizgiyi aştığını ve İran'la müzakerelerin giderek zorlaştığını söyledi.
The Chinese tanker Rich Starry defied the U.S. blockade by transiting the Strait of Hormuz on April 14. A political scientist told Brasil de Fato that the U.S. has crossed a red line, making negotiations with Iran increasingly difficult.
SAVE Yasası, Kongre'nin şimdiye kadar geçirdiği en kısıtlayıcı oylama yasası olarak tanımlandı

▸ sentez / synthesis
Brennan Center CEO'su Michael Waldman, Amerikan Seçmen Uygunluğunu Koruma (SAVE) Yasası'nı Kongre'nin şimdiye kadar geçirdiği en kısıtlayıcı oylama yasası olarak tanımlıyor ve seçimleri Cumhuriyetçilere fayda sağlayacak şekilde federalleştirdiğini söylüyor.
The Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act is described by Brennan Center CEO Michael Waldman as the most restrictive voting bill ever passed by Congress, federalizing elections in a way that could benefit Republicans.

CounterPunch reports that the collapse of US-Iran talks in Islamabad and Washington's announcement of a maritime blockade of the Strait of Hormuz signal a shift toward a new diplomatic order led by the Global South.

CounterPunch reports that the Trump administration signed a Memorandum of Understanding on March 31, 2026, mandating prioritization of livestock grazing on public lands. The article argues this is ecologically damaging.

Energy Secretary Chris Wright said gas prices may not return below $3 until next year, after the midterm elections, causing panic among Republicans. Trump had previously claimed he could end the war quickly.

Surveillance company Palantir has released a 22-point manifesto lamenting the post-war 'neutering' of Nazi Germany. The company has seen its value explode as it scores massive contracts from the Trump administration.

BreakThrough News' Rania Khalek analyzes Trump's April 16 announcement of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon. The ceasefire was announced a week after Israel's 'Black Wednesday' massacre in Beirut and Southern Lebanon, generating mixed reactions.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called Israel the only country in the Middle East where the Christian 'standard of living' is growing. This statement came after Israel bombed countless churches, according to Truthout.

2 src · 2 it2026-04-20 20:25
Asia Times · DropSite News
▸ sentez / synthesis

DropSite News, sızdırılan belgelerin ABD-İran görüşmeleri sırasında gündeme gelen gizli bir Suudi Arabistan-Pakistan karşılıklı savunma paktının ayrıntılarını ortaya koyduğunu ekliyor.
Asia Times reports Iran is considering attending another round of peace talks with the Trump administration in Islamabad, despite accusing the US of maximalist demands. DropSite News adds that leaked documents reveal details of a secret Saudi Arabia-Pakistan mutual defense pact, which came into focus amid the US-Iran talks.

A JPMorganChase data center received a $77 million tax break under a program designed to attract job-creating companies, but it promises to create only one job, according to Truthout.

Historian Ruth Ben-Ghiat argues that President Donald Trump's actions align with the characteristics of fascist dictators, as reported by AlterNet. She points to patterns of grandiosity and authoritarian behavior.

The Chicago Teachers Union is leading a May 1 International Workers' Day shutdown, calling for a national day of action under the banner 'no work, no school, no shopping' to protest the Trump administration. CTU president Stacy Davis Gates discussed the plans with BreakThrough News.

Amos Hochstein, a top Biden administration official, expressed support for Trump's attack on Iran, stating that Democratic officials simulated attacks on Iran because that may have had to happen under their watch.

Environmental groups have appealed to the California Supreme Court to overturn a new rooftop solar policy that slashes credits for new solar users.

Drop Site News, ABD-İran ateşkesinin görüşmeler askıdayken sona ermek üzere olduğunu ve Louisiana'da bir silahlı saldırganın sekiz çocuğu öldürdüğünü bildiriyor. Ayrıca İsrail'in Lübnan'da Gazze tarzı 'Sarı Hat' uygulamasına dikkat çekiliyor.
Drop Site News reports that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is set to expire with talks in limbo, and a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children. Also noted is Israel's Gaza-style 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon.

▸ sentez / synthesis
Stanley Carpenter, 24 yaşındaki oğlu Jalen Carpenter'ın 15 Nisan'da Illinois Eyalet Polisi tarafından vurularak öldürüldüğünü söyledi. Oğlunu çalışkan ve 'kocaman bir oyuncak ayı' olarak tanımladı.
Stanley Carpenter said his son Jalen Carpenter, 24, was shot and killed by Illinois State Police on April 15. He described his son as hardworking and 'a big teddy bear'.

Drop Site News reports that a senior Iranian official said if Trump starts bombing again, Iran will cut off all diplomatic channels and focus on fighting, warning of significantly greater costs to the U.S.
İsrail ateşkese rağmen güney Lübnan'daki yıkımı yoğunlaştırdı; ihbarcı Gazze yardım bölgesine saldırı iddia etti

Truthout · DropSite News
▸ sentez / synthesis
Truthout, İsrail güçlerinin ateşkese rağmen 2 Mart'tan bu yana güney Lübnan'da günde 1.000'den fazla evi yok ettiğini bildiriyor. DropSite News, ikinci bir müteahhit olan David McIntosh'un İsrail askerlerinin Gazze'de yardım arayan kalabalıklara ateş açtığını iddia etmek için ortaya çıktığını ekliyor.
Truthout reports Israeli forces have destroyed over 1,000 homes daily in southern Lebanon since March 2, despite a ceasefire. DropSite News adds that a second contractor, David McIntosh, has come forward to allege Israeli soldiers fired on crowds seeking aid in Gaza.
Viktor Orbán 16 yıl sonra görevden alındı; yeni lider yolsuzlukla mücadele reformları sözü verdi

3 src · 3 it2026-04-19 15:47
Truthout · Jacobin · OCCRP
▸ sentez / synthesis
Truthout, Macarların otoriter yönetimi reddetmesiyle Viktor Orbán'ın 16 yıl sonra görevden alındığını bildiriyor. Jacobin, Péter Magyar'ın Tisza partisinin parlamentoda üçte iki süper çoğunluk kazandığını ekliyor. OCCRP, Macaristan'ın yeni liderinin yeni denetim kurumları kurarak 'endüstriyel ölçekteki' yolsuzluğu ortadan kaldırmayı planladığını bildiriyor.
Truthout reports that Viktor Orbán has been ousted after 16 years as Hungarians rejected authoritarian rule. Jacobin adds that Péter Magyar's Tisza party won a two-thirds supermajority in parliament. OCCRP reports that Hungary's incoming leader plans to dismantle 'industrial-scale' corruption by establishing new watchdog agencies.

Truthout reports that Palestinian digital autonomy is constrained by settler colonialism and neoliberal privatization, with Israeli censorship and surveillance algorithms limiting online expression.

A Brooklyn exhibition in collaboration with Mariame Kaba features paintings about prisons, incarceration, and abolition, aiming to expose the realities of mass incarceration to a wider audience.

Common Dreams reports that Republican lawmakers have introduced federal legislation that would give oil and gas companies immunity from laws and lawsuits holding them accountable for climate change. Eleven states and dozens of local governments have sued the industry.

The battle for Bint Jbeil is highlighted as a symbolic resistance point where Israeli forces face setbacks.

DropSite News reports that as international attention has shifted to Iran and Lebanon, Israel has continued its assault on Gaza, killing more children. The article highlights the ongoing toll on civilians.

ProPublica reports that Omaha, Nebraska, is home to a massive Superfund site, yet most children living there are not tested for lead contamination. The articles highlight the health risks and lack of testing.
İsrail Batı Şeria'da yerleşimleri genişletiyor ve İran sinagogunu bombalıyor
Israel expands West Bank settlements and bombs Iranian synagogue

Electronic Intifada · The Real News Network
▸ sentez / synthesis
İsrail, Batı Şeria'da özel mülkiyete ait Filistin topraklarında yeni yerleşimler onayladı; bu, Nakba'dan bu yana en büyük toprak gasbı olarak nitelendiriliyor. Ayrıca İsrail, Hamursuz Bayramı sırasında Tahran'daki bir sinagogu bombaladı ve Yahudi hedeflerine saldırması nedeniyle eleştirildi.
Israel has approved new settlements on privately owned Palestinian land in the West Bank, described as the largest land grab since the Nakba. Separately, Israel bombed a synagogue in Tehran during Passover, drawing criticism for targeting Jewish sites.

Democratic senators overwhelmingly reject arms sales to Israel in symbolic vote

DropSite News ve Zeteo anketi, Michigan'daki Demokrat Senato seçmenlerinin AIPAC'ın etkisini sınırlamayı Hasan Piker'dan daha fazla önemsediğini gösteriyor.
The Intercept reports that bills blocking arms sales to Israel found widespread Democratic support despite being defeated by Senate Republicans. A DropSite News and Zeteo poll indicates Democratic Senate voters in Michigan care more about limiting AIPAC's influence than about Hasan Piker.

Common Dreams reports that Patriotic Millionaires, along with several U.S. senators and representatives, gathered at the Capitol to demand Congress raise taxes on the wealthy to address economic inequality.

A Senate vote to rein in President Trump's authority to wage war on Iran failed, with 52 senators voting against. The vote occurred despite Trump's threat that 'a whole civilization will die.'

Common Dreams reports that polls by Data for Progress show a majority of voters in Arizona, California, Colorado, and Michigan support Senate resolutions to block the delivery of 12,000 1,000-pound bombs to Israel.

UN experts have called for a global halt to arms transfers to Israel, stating that attacks on Lebanon violate the UN Charter and are not self-defense.

The House postponed a vote on renewing Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act after bipartisan privacy advocates opposed the measure. The White House and intelligence agencies had pushed for renewal.

The ACLU has alleged that guards at Alligator Alcatraz jail beat prisoners who requested phone access, obstructing a court order. The case is before a judge.

Big Oil gains $30 million per hour in windfall profits from Iran war

The oil and gas industry has earned an additional $234 billion in profits in the first month of Trump's war on Iran, equivalent to $30 million per hour.

A report from the Center for Economic and Policy Research examines the sovereign debt trap cycle and calls for systemic reforms. As of March 2026, 75 of 119 low- and middle-income countries face debt distress.

US soldiers are questioning the wars they are ordered to fight, with a history of dissent against military engagements. The article highlights a crisis of conscience among troops due to Trump's extreme use of the military.

Liberation News reports that communities across the U.S. are protesting the use of mass surveillance technologies, linking them to mass deportation and racist policing, with a call to 'Get Flock off our streets'.

A plant explosion in Louisiana released toxic substances, with residents reporting black rain and long-term health concerns. The incident has raised alarms about environmental contamination.

ProPublica reports that Trump's Memphis Crime Task Force arrested over 800 immigrants, but only 2% of the arrests were for violent crimes, according to records.

The Intercept reports that an armed off-duty Phoenix police officer attempted to incite violence at a high school anti-ICE protest. The officer told fellow police, 'My plan is legitimately to just let them all assault me and you guys arrest them all.'

The Intercept reports that former Representative Swalwell's rapid collapse over sexual assault allegations signals that Democrats have learned that voters see accountability for abuse as a key electoral issue.

The Intercept reports that grassroots opposition to renewing Section 702 of FISA is growing, partly due to fears about AI used to sort Americans' data, but Democratic leaders are not rallying the caucus against it.

US routinely kills civilians in boat strikes in Latin America, reports say

The Real News Network, Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın bir insan hakları izleme kuruluşunu ölümcül, yasadışı tekne saldırıları kampanyasını görmezden gelmeye ittiğini, Pentagon yetkililerinin ise kara saldırılarına destek ifade ettiğini ekliyor.
The Intercept reports that the Trump administration has hit its 50th strike on a civilian boat in Latin American waters. The Real News Network adds that the State Department pushed a human rights watchdog to ignore the deadly, illegal boat strike campaign, while Pentagon officials expressed support for strikes on land.

The Intercept reports that Meta has implemented new rules allowing it to ban users or suppress comments that include the word 'antifa' alongside 'content-level threat signals'.

DropSite News reports that prominent journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin has been in jail for six weeks in Kuwait and faces trial in a special tribunal. His arrest is part of a wider crackdown on online speech in Gulf countries during the Iran war.

The Intercept reports that investigative journalist Amy Goodman, host of 'Democracy Now!', discusses the media's 'access of evil' in a new documentary, emphasizing the need for independent media.

A commentary piece criticizes the political spectacle, including threats, botches, and media sanewashing, amid failed ceasefire and peace talks.

The US Navy has imposed a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route. Reports indicate the operation cost over $300 million and Iran claims to have foiled an attempted rescue mission. Analysts suggest Iran is gaining an upper hand in the conflict.

Liberation News reports that Da'Quain Johnson was killed by Grand Rapids police, marking the 12th officer-involved shooting in the city in four years.

The Real News Network reviews Frank Bardacke's history of the Farm Workers union, arguing that a lack of democracy in the UFW gave Cesar Chavez immunity and contributed to the union's decline.

Two companies contracted to build a billion-dollar ICE detention center in Texas have a history of legal problems, regulatory violations, and deadly conditions. One was fired and was never registered in Texas.

US-Iran tensions escalate amid failed talks and military incidents

The Real News Network · Investig'Action · Investig'Action · Bellingcat · The Grayzone
▸ sentez / synthesis
İran Dışişleri Bakanı Abbas Araghchi, Trump yönetiminin anlaşmaya yaklaşılan nükleer görüşmeleri sabote ettiğini söyledi. Bu arada, ABD'nin bir İran köyüne döşenebilir mayınlar attığı ve birkaç kişinin öldüğü bildiriliyor. Soruşturmalar ayrıca İsrail ve FBI'ın Trump'ı İran'la savaşa kışkırtmak için suikast planları manipüle ettiğini öne sürüyor.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that the Trump administration sabotaged nuclear talks that were close to a deal. Meanwhile, reports indicate that the US may have deployed scatterable mines over an Iranian village, killing several people. Investigations also suggest that Israel and the FBI manipulated assassination plots to provoke Trump into war with Iran.

The State Department told the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights that it lacks competence to review Trump's campaign of deadly boat strikes. The Intercept reported the statement.

Voters in Hungary ended 16 consecutive years of far-right rule under Viktor Orbán, with the European Union head stating that Europe has always chosen Hungary.

Elon Musk'ın iş gücü tasfiyesinde işten çıkarılan dışişleri memurları, Dışişleri Bakanlığı'nın Orta Doğu'da mahsur kalan Amerikalılara yardım edemediği konusunda uyarıyor. The Intercept haberi bildirdi.
Foreign service officers fired in Elon Musk's workforce purge warn the State Department is unable to help Americans stranded in the Middle East. The Intercept reported the story.

The Grayzone reports that six Palestine Action activists face a retrial after acquittal in February, and could be sentenced as terrorists. The jury has not been informed of the 'terrorist' designation.

The Department of Justice is moving forward with a settlement in a predatory lending case that a judge worried does not do enough to help victims. Critics called the settlement a slap in the face.

On April 10, 2006, millions of immigrants marched across the US in one of the largest protest days in modern history, leading up to a May 1 general strike.

Mexico enacts universal healthcare as US moves opposite direction

Medicare for All advocate Wendell Potter said it is both inspiring and frustrating to see other nations advance public healthcare while the US dismantles its own. The Real News Network reported his comments.

The Real News Network reported that $4,049 of the average US taxpayer's bill last year went to war and weaponry, quoting a critic who said tax dollars bomb children abroad rather than feed and educate children at home.
